easy: Ace: to excel at something; also a playing card or tennis serve.
Eat: to consume food; to corrode or wear away.
Top: the highest point; to surpass or exceed.
Ran: past tense of run; moved swiftly on foot.
Cat: a small domesticated carnivorous mammal.
Tap: to strike lightly; a device controlling flow.
Rats: small rodents; also slang for betrayers.
Step: a movement of the foot; a stage in a process.
Wolf: a wild carnivorous mammal; to eat greedily.
Able: having the power or skill to do something.
West: the direction of the setting sun.
Lake: a large body of water surrounded by land.
Care: concern for someone; to look after.
King: a male monarch; the most powerful chess piece.
Lump: a compact mass; to group together.
Mist: thin fog; a fine spray of liquid.
medium: Croak: the sound a frog makes; to die (slang).
Store: a place to buy goods; to keep for future use.
Chase: to pursue; an act of running after someone.
Thick: having a large distance between surfaces; dense.
Place: a particular area; to put something somewhere.
Range: the area covered; a series between limits.
Right: correct; the direction opposite to left.
Clock: a device that measures time.
Spare: to refrain from harming; extra, not used.
Sharp: having a fine edge; mentally quick.
Block: a solid piece; to obstruct or impede.
Faults: flaws or errors; a crack in rock strata.
Wafer: a thin crisp biscuit; a thin disk used in technology.
Reign: the period of a monarch's rule; to hold power.
Trick: a clever act; to deceive someone.
Mates: companions or friends; checkmates in chess.
hard: Plates: flat dishes; tectonic slabs of Earth's crust.
Groan: a deep sound of pain or unhappiness.
Retain: to keep possession of; to remember.
Greet: to welcome or acknowledge someone's arrival.
Master: an expert; to acquire complete skill in.
Bloats: swells with liquid or gas; inflates abnormally.
Puzzle: a problem for creative thinking; to confuse.
Grapes: small juicy fruits used to make wine.
Makers: people who create or manufacture things.
Crafts: skilled handwork; to make with skill.
Plants: living organisms that photosynthesize; factories.
Stamps: marks made by pressing; postage labels.
Sprint: to run at full speed; a short fast race.
Garble: to mix up a message until it is confused.
Snorts: makes a sharp sound through the nose.
